doc.title: Convert preferred stock to common
doc.event: Holders of 1,000 shares of convertible preferred ($100 par, originally issued at 105) convert into 8,000 shares of $5-par common. The preferred's BOOK VALUE — par plus its own paid-in capital — rolls into the common accounts. Equity to equity, no gain, no loss.

variants.label: Book value of the preferred moves across

entries.caption: 1,000 preferred ($100 par, issued at 105) → 8,000 common ($5 par)

lines.label: 1,000 × $100 par off
lines.why: The preferred's par capital leaves its account.
lines.trap: Conversion removes the ORIGINAL issuance amounts — today's market prices for either stock are spectators.

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Preferred
lines.label: its original excess
lines.why: The preferred's book value includes the PIC created when it was issued (1,000 × $5 excess) — it converts too.
lines.trap: Leaving the preferred's PIC behind strands orphaned capital under a share class that no longer exists.

lines.label: 8,000 × $5 par
lines.trap: Par of the COMMON now — don't reuse the preferred's $100 par anywhere on the credit side.

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common
lines.label: the plug
lines.trap: No income-statement account can appear — equity-for-equity swaps never create gains or losses.

computationPaths.narration: Book value of the preferred = par 100,000 + its PIC 5,000 = 105,000. That value re-dresses as common: par 40,000, PIC plug 65,000.

memorize.body: Convert preferred: debit its par AND its original PIC (the full book value); credit common par × new shares; plug to PIC—Common.
Rare twist: if the COMMON par exceeds the preferred's book value, the shortfall debits RETAINED EARNINGS — never a loss account.

questions.prompt: Preferred (1,000 shares, $100 par, issued at 105) converts to 8,000 shares of $5-par common. PIC in Excess of Par — Common is credited for…

distractors.feedback: 60,000 converts only the preferred's PAR (100,000). Its book value includes the original 5,000 issuance excess — 105,000 converts, so the plug is 65,000.

accountBank.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Preferred
Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common

build.scaffold: Total the preferred's book value first (par + its PIC). Debit both pieces off, credit common par, plug PIC—Common. Three of the seven bank accounts never belong here.
